comment.body:
Fed was owned :)) ...again
I show you something important.
Here I coded tech 20d rolling alpha and you can see the alpha's ROC with its weekly avg. 
The 1 week change is -15.41% despite the SPX slow positive drift. But look, despite the very last upward move in the alpha curve looks small, its weight is bigger than it seems. We should see NVDA and AMD alpha keeping a positive trend into OpEx. (NVDA doesn't look too good) Otherwise it is actually a temporal positive correction.
The question is if this correction's gonna hold into Friday or into month end. (My estimated odds lean towards suppressive post-OpEx, but we need to keep our eyes open).

I will cover this in the coming post, but don't forget that stagflation is still there and the dovish tone doesn't make good for the inflationary pressures! Trump doesn't want another cycle. 
The net downward move is not done yet. Wider bias remains. I use these positive corrections to scale into puts as I kept saying it before.

comment.body: alpha is the return difference between a stock or stock portfolio minus the market return (beta). So practically that tells you if a stock or portfolio outperformed the market over a certain period. Here that period is rolling, so it's always the last n days for example
comment.raw_body: alpha is the return difference between a stock or stock portfolio minus the market return (beta). So practically that tells you if a stock or portfolio outperformed the market over a certain period. Here that period is rolling, so it's always the last n days for example

comment.body: i believe it refers to an options postion that is in the money...say 80 delta.  you would sell the 80 delta profitable option and 'roll' down to, say, a 30 delta
comment.raw_body: i believe it refers to an options postion that is in the money...say 80 delta.  you would sell the 80 delta profitable option and 'roll' down to, say, a 30 delta
